frozen_components: Seed 1.5-VL (used for data cleaning, not trained in this work)
Unified multimodal models (Qwen-Image, GPT-Image-1) in TTS loop
architecture.summary: OmniVerifier is a generative verifier system that audits visual outcomes (generated images, intermediate visual states) during multimodal reasoning and generation. The system consists of three components: (1) ViVerBench, a 3,594-sample benchmark for evaluating visual verification across 16 tasks in 6 categories; (2) OmniVerifier-7B, a 7B-parameter vision-language model trained via RL (DAPO) on Qwen2.5-VL-7B to perform binary true/false judgments with textual explanations on image-prompt alignment; (3) OmniVerifier-TTS, a sequential test-time scaling loop where OmniVerifier judges a generated image, outputs an edit prompt if misaligned, and feeds that prompt back to a unified multimodal model for iterative refinement. The seam is text: verifications and edit guidance cross the model boundary as natural language tokens.
trained_components: Task-specific verifiers for maze and robotics (world-modeling)

biblio.title: Generative Universal Verifier as Multimodal Meta-Reasoner

claims.statement: OmniVerifier-7B achieves an 8.3% overall performance enhancement on ViVerBench rule-based evaluation, surpassing GPT-4o and achieving capabilities comparable to Qwen2.5-VL-72B.
OmniVerifier-TTS achieves +3.7 improvement on T2I-ReasonBench on Qwen-Image baseline.
OmniVerifier-TTS achieves +4.3 improvement on GenEval++ on Qwen-Image baseline.
ViVerBench comprises 3,594 data samples curated through manual annotation by 12 domain experts, expert review, and human evaluation across 16 tasks in 6 categories.

Three fundamental atomic capabilities underlying visual-outcome verification are identified: explicit alignment, relational verification, and integrative reasoning, which mutually generalize and interact synergistically via reinforcement learning.

State-of-the-art VLMs (including Gemini 2.5 Pro, Seed 1.5-VL) exhibit three general limitations on visual verification: weakness in fine-grained and challenging image-prompt alignment, mismatched representation of world knowledge, and underdeveloped critics for visual reasoning tasks.

Sequential test-time scaling (OmniVerifier-TTS) outperforms parallel test-time scaling (Best-of-N) across reasoning-based and compositional-based generation benchmarks, while requiring approximately 47% fewer generation steps per prompt.

Training on maze verification data alone exhibits minimal generalization to other tasks, attributed to sparse and discrete synthetic patterns contrasting sharply with natural images, creating a significant distribution gap that offers limited transferable signal.
